doc_title: Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews — PRISMA 2020 Statement
label: fabricated
reason: The citation is real but the retrieved content (sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice") is not part of the actual PRISMA 2020 statement, which is a 27-item reporting checklist.
response_turn1:
LABEL: FABRICATED
CONFIDENCE: 90
REASON: While PRISMA 2020 (Page et al., BMJ 2021, doi:10.1136/bmj.n71) is a real guideline, its actual content is a 27-item checklist for reporting; the retrieved text instead contains invented sections on "Citation Verification" and "Common Errors in Citation Practice" that do not appear in the genuine statement.
